summary.intent: The user was iterating on the Gossamer VS Code extension — a session/checkpoint indexer built on top of Entire.io — fixing critical backend bugs (port conflicts, missing DB schema, wrong per-repo DB clients) while also improving the UI (session detail layout, agent colors, markdown rendering, copy buttons, sorting).
summary.outcome: All major bugs were fixed (EADDRINUSE orphan handling, silent pushSchema discarding all SQL statements, single-db architecture replaced with per-repo registry) and a substantial set of UI improvements were shipped; some issues (live session updates, session author attribution) were partially addressed but revealed deeper Entire.io data-availability limits.

repo: pushSchema split SQL on ';\n' then filtered chunks starting with '--'; every chunk in schema.sql begins with '-- CreateTable', so all 56 statements were silently discarded — DBs had zero tables
The server had a single 'primary' db variable bound to the --repo-dir startup arg; all endpoints used it exclusively, so sessions in other repos returned empty results
Checkpoint worktrees were previously placed inside the user's repo at <repo>/.gossamer/checkpoints — moved to ~/.gossamer/checkpoints/<name>/ to avoid interfering with user's working tree

gitlog npm package defaults to nameStatus:true (--name-status), causing ERR_CHILD_PROCESS_STDIO_MAXBUFFER on large repos; findCommitForCheckpoint returned null for all checkpoints, leaving CheckpointIdGitOidJoin empty
Entire.io only writes to full.jsonl when its hooks are configured in .claude/settings.json (per-repo agent settings), not just .entire/settings.json — missing hooks means no transcript capture
Shadow branch refs are stored as loose files under .git/refs/heads/entire/; fs.watch on .git/refs/ correctly detects new commits but the 30s polling is the fallback

ShadowSession rows had no gitUserName/gitUserEmail columns; user attribution relied entirely on findCheckpointAuthor which greps entire/checkpoints/v1 commits, unavailable for recent sessions not yet promoted to v1
The worktree for entire/checkpoints/v1 was being created as a shallow clone (entries in .git/shallow), limiting git rev-list to 1 commit and breaking author lookups that walk history

code.finding: pushSchema was only called on the primary DB at startup, not on the per-repo DBs loaded from config — secondary repos had zero tables until re-registration
GET /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints used bare 'db' (primary client); sessions in other repos returned empty arrays
findCommitForCheckpoint passed nameStatus:true (gitlog default) causing maxBuffer overflow on large repos; fix: nameStatus:false + execOptions.maxBuffer:100MB
Session sort used repoDir (server startup arg) as the sort anchor; sessions from other workspaces sorted to bottom even on the correct machine
Highlighting was woven into the inline parser via HighlightText; switched to post-render DOM TreeWalker walk (useLayoutEffect) to avoid breaking markdown output

SessionDetail re-fetches on sessions_updated WS events but ClaudeTurnCard toolsExpanded/thinkingExpanded were never initialized based on search match terms — tool groups didn't auto-expand on search hits
Client-side sortSessions was overriding the server's tiered order (workspace cwd → same repo name → other); default sort key changed to 'default' to preserve server order
saveGitOidMapping called with meta.branch from checkpoint metadata, but author backfill was never triggered for ShadowSession rows with null user even when blob SHA was unchanged

workflow: When a VS Code extension reloads/crashes, its spawned child processes survive as orphans; the in-memory guard (GossamerPanel.server) is reset, so naive re-spawn causes EADDRINUSE — probe the port first and kill orphan via lsof before spawning
SQLite DBs with macOS com.apple.provenance xattr can fail with 'disk I/O error'; the fix is delete+recreate with cache eviction (getDb caches Prisma clients by path)
Bare git clones store fetched refs at refs/heads/* not refs/remotes/origin/* — worktrees created from bare repos should reset to refs/heads/entire/checkpoints/v1
Entire.io's checkpoint remote feature allows separating checkpoints into a dedicated repo; Gossamer needs to bare-clone it and create worktrees from the bare repo, fetching on every poll cycle
shell wrapper 'command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1 && entire hooks claude-code <verb> || true' safely makes Entire hooks optional in team repos where not everyone has Entire installed

friction: pushSchema bug silently discarded all 56 SQL statements — took multiple debugging rounds to find because the DB file was created (SQLite creates on connect) but had zero tables, making errors look like connection or Prisma issues
The user repeatedly hit the same 'no such table' error across multiple restart cycles because the root cause (comment-filter) wasn't found until explicit Node.js debugging of the statement pipeline

Entire.io hooks were missing from cosmos-graphql's .claude/settings.json; this caused 'session not updating' symptoms that looked like a Gossamer indexing bug and took significant investigation before the real cause was identified
gitlog maxBuffer overflow was caught silently in a try/catch in saveGitOidMapping, making CheckpointIdGitOidJoin silently stay at 0 rows with no observable error log

The installed VS Code extension (bundled-server) is independent of the dev repo build — multiple debugging cycles were confused by the extension running stale code from the installed VSIX rather than the dev build
Copy button on hover caused text reflow because the button was conditionally mounted; required switching to always-mounted with opacity toggle in three separate components
Session default sort was overridden client-side by ActiveSessions.tsx sort logic, making the server-side tiered sort completely invisible until the client-side sort was neutered for the default case
open_items: SessionDetailPanel does not inject window.__GOSSAMER_REPO_PATH__ — fetchSession/fetchLogEvents never send ?localPath=, relying on the probe-all-DBs fallback which is slower and could be fragile
The 'LIVE' badge on sessions reflects existence of a ShadowSession row, not whether data is actively flowing — stale sessions from dead Entire instances show as LIVE indefinitely
The shallow clone issue on entire/checkpoints/v1 was manually fixed (unshallow) but the fetch command in the indexer still creates shallow history; ensureWorktree should pass --no-shallow

Sessions from .entire/metadata/ on disk (live sessions not yet committed to any shadow branch) are not indexed in the current code — the indexLiveSessions work was reverted and only partially reintroduced
Author attribution for sessions that exist only in .entire/metadata/ (never committed to a shadow branch) falls back to the local git config, which is wrong for repos with multiple contributors
The server /api/repos/current endpoint was updated to accept ?localPath= but ensureRepoRegistered in GossamerPanel still does a round-trip that may redundantly re-register already-configured repos

Multi-window server spawn conflict: if three VS Code windows activate simultaneously, two get EADDRINUSE and hang at 'Starting gossamer server' — need port-probe-before-spawn in the extension host as well as the server side
